classification.analysis: Labour has collapsed to third place behind Reform UK after devastating May 2026 local elections. Over 83 MPs demand Starmer's resignation, Health Secretary Streeting quit to challenge him, and UK borrowing costs hit 30-year highs. Consumer confidence plunged to lowest since October 2023 amid Middle East war energy shock.

Merz faces historic crisis one year into office: his 15% approval is the lowest ever for a German chancellor, AfD has overtaken his coalition in polls, and 55% of Germans expect early government collapse. US-Germany tensions over Iran policy triggered troop withdrawals and tariff threats, while consumer confidence plunged to a three-year low amid energy price shocks and economic contraction.

Macron's approval has collapsed to 18%, tying historic lows, while far-right RN leads polls at 35% ahead of 2027 elections. Budget passed in February after no-confidence votes failed, but May Day protests drew 300,000 demanding workers' rights as Bardella campaigns aggressively.

Sánchez battles corruption charges against his wife and inner circle while Vox surges from 12.4% to 17.7%. His anti-war stance on Iran and Israel won unexpected regional gains, but Junts withdrew coalition support and polls show the PSOE-Sumar bloc losing its majority. The far-right is consolidating.

Coalition talks collapsed in early May after six weeks, with kingmaker Løkke Rasmussen backing rival Poulsen to lead negotiations—a major blow to Frederiksen. Her coalition suffered historic losses in March, falling from 50% to 40%, while Danish People's Party tripled support to 9%. Consumer confidence plunged to -18.6 in April post-election.

One Nation's surge from 6% to 26% in one year represents Australia's fastest-ever far-right rise. After historic May 2025 landslide, Albanese faces 59% disapproval following broken housing tax promises and One Nation's shocking Farrer by-election victory. Consumer confidence collapsed amid US-Iran tensions affecting fuel supplies. Budget controversy deepens crisis.

Martin faces mounting pressure ahead of critical by-elections where Fianna Fáil polls at just 4% in Dublin Central. Coalition support has collapsed from 42.7% at election to 33.7% now, with Sinn Féin leading nationally. Consumer confidence hit a three-year low in April. Leadership questions swirl around this weekend's centenary Ard Fheis, though no far-right threat exists in Irish politics.

Jetten's minority coalition faces mounting pressure just three months in, with consumer confidence hitting a four-month low in March and controversial austerity plans—including raising retirement age and healthcare deductibles—sparking harsh opposition criticism. The coalition holds only 66 of 150 seats while far-right forces command 42, creating structural instability.

Montenegro's minority government faces severe pressure as coalition polling collapsed from 32% to 23%, now virtually tied with far-right Chega at 22.9%. The home affairs minister resigned in February after criticism of Storm Kristin response, while AD's presidential candidate polled just 11% in January. Government passes legislation only with Chega support on immigration, fueling far-right normalization amid housing crisis backlash.

Labour won September 2025 election but Progress Party doubled vote share to 24%. Current polls show Progress leading at 29% versus Labour's 22%, reversing February momentum. Consumer confidence improved from historic lows but remains negative. Trump's Greenland letter and Middle East tensions dominate recent diplomatic agenda. Coalition faces certain defeat if election held today.

Takaichi delivered Japan's largest postwar electoral victory in February 2026, winning 316 seats and forming a supermajority coalition with JIP. Far-right Sanseito gained ground to 15 seats but remains marginal. Consumer confidence has declined sharply since her honeymoon peak, dropping from 40.0 to 32.2 amid Middle East tensions and oil supply concerns, testing her economic management.

Reform's coalition has imploded, plunging from 58% at the 2023 election to 29% now. Michal expelled the Social Democrats in March 2025 amid record-low approval. The coalition lost badly in October 2025 local elections. Conservative Isamaa now leads polls at 26%, while Reform polls at historic lows. Michal pushes deficit reduction amid fiscal warnings from the central bank.

Kristersson's coalition faces electoral wipeout in September, polling at just 25.5% versus 29% in 2022. Consumer confidence plunged to 91.5 in April amid unemployment fears and Iran war impacts on energy prices. The Liberals' March agreement eliminating red lines with Sweden Democrats triggered internal defections but failed to arrest the bloc's collapse against surging Social Democrats at 33%.

Orpo's coalition with far-right Finns Party has lost 11 points since 2023 election, now polling at just 35.5% of seats. Consumer confidence hit 15-month low in January. Government rocked by racist social media scandal involving Finns MPs in January 2026 and ministerial resignation in February. Finance Ministry warned of €1.4bn EU deficit breach risk in December.

Mitsotakis governs with a majority but faces mounting pressure from wiretapping and OPEKEPE scandals, the Tempi tragedy, and inflation concerns tied to Iran conflict. New Democracy has dropped from 41% to 30% while Greek Solution doubled to 9.5%. Opposition remains fragmented across PASOK, SYRIZA, and emerging Tsipras/Karystianou movements, allowing ND to maintain control despite erosion.

Stocker's three-party coalition faces mounting crisis. Governing parties have plunged from 56.5% to 46.5% since September 2024 while FPÖ surges to 36.4%. Deeply unpopular €15 billion austerity program disproportionately hits lower-income households amid Austria's longest recession since 1945. Coalition clings to razor-thin majority as far-right consolidates dominance.

De Wever's five-party coalition maintains parliamentary majority but faces mounting pressure. A nationwide general strike on May 12 targeted his government's reforms. Vlaams Belang polls at 16% nationally, up from 13.3% in 2024, though the cordon sanitaire holds. Recent diplomatic tensions with the US and coalition strain over Russia policy add turbulence to his second year.

Luxon's coalition teeters on a four-seat majority after National collapsed from 38% to 30%. NZ First surged to 11.7%, becoming kingmaker. Consumer confidence cratered during the Iran war fuel crisis. Luxon survived an April leadership challenge amid public crisis talks with Peters over foreign policy. Coalition fractures widen six months before the November election as minor partners prepare to differentiate themselves after Budget Day.

Meloni's far-right government, 3.5 years in power, faces mounting pressures. Culture Ministry turmoil reflects broader institutional reshaping efforts. Coalition polling has slipped below majority threshold at 41.8%, down from 43% at 2022 election. Constitutional referendum on judicial reform and diplomatic tensions with Trump over Iran War mark an active phase of governance challenges amid warnings that 2026 will be tougher than 2025.

Babiš governs with far-right SPD and Motorists in formal coalition holding 108/200 seats. Coalition polling declined 3.4 points since October election. Massive protests erupted in March with 250,000 demonstrating against government. Open conflict with President Pavel emerged over ministerial appointments and Ukraine policy. Government cancelled euro readiness reports, signaling eurosceptic turn.

Trump's approval has plunged from 47% at inauguration to 37% amid the Iran war quagmire that has spiked gas prices and economic anxiety. His China summit May 13-15 failed to break the Iran deadlock, leaving Republicans nervous about November midterms. Consumer confidence remains depressed as gasoline prices and tariff concerns persist.

Magyar sworn in May 9 after historic landslide ending Orbán's 16-year rule. EU flag reinstated in parliament. Consumer confidence surged 19 points to 5.5-year high. Post-election polling shows Tisza above 57%, more than double Fidesz's 23%. Constitutional reform and anti-corruption office planned. Honeymoon peak.

Fico's Moscow visit on May 9 made him the only EU leader to attend Victory Day celebrations, deepening Slovakia's diplomatic isolation. Coalition polling has collapsed from 43% at election to 29%, while mass protests continue against democratic backsliding. Consumer confidence hit a three-year low in March amid fiscal austerity and political polarization heading into 2027 elections.

Milei's approval has collapsed from 53% at inauguration to 35% amid corruption scandals involving Chief of Staff Manuel Adorni and falling real wages. Massive university protests erupted May 12 as economic pain intensifies despite macro improvements. Opposition remains fragmented, with far-left Myriam Bregman polling ahead of Peronist leader Axel Kicillof.

Netanyahu's coalition faces imminent collapse after ultra-Orthodox party UTJ called for Knesset dissolution on May 12 when he refused to advance Haredi military draft exemption before elections. Coalition polling shows steep decline from 64 to 51-54 seats. Nationwide protests erupted May 16 against anti-democratic legislation as political crisis deepens ahead of October election deadline.

Erdoğan maintains power through intensified authoritarian crackdown, imprisoning hundreds of opposition figures including leading presidential rival Imamoglu after March 2024 electoral defeat. Approval rebounded to 46% amid institutional destruction and judicial capture, while pursuing Kurdish peace process partly to attract voters ahead of 2028 elections.

Tusk's coalition holds steady at 54% but the far-right has nearly doubled to 13%, with radical KKP adding another 11%. Trump's troop withdrawal sparked Tusk-opposition clashes over NATO solidarity. PiS collapses to 24%, fracturing internally while far-right consolidates. Consumer confidence deteriorating. Institutional gridlock deepens as President Nawrocki vetoes prolifically. Coalition fatigue mounting before 2027 election.

Carney secured parliamentary majority through April byelections, extending his honeymoon. He's pivoting Canada toward Europe while managing Trump tariffs and striking oil pipeline deal with Alberta. The far-right PPC remains collapsed at 1%. His 56% approval and 10-point Liberal lead reflect successful positioning as change from Trudeau era.

Lee's approval has dipped to 61% from 64% at inauguration, with critics citing excessive welfare spending and legal controversies. Democratic Party leads strongly ahead of June 3 local elections. Economy shows mixed signals: strong chip exports and stock market gains offset by inflation concerns and rising oil prices. Consumer confidence remains above neutral at 110.8 but down from November's eight-year high. Party leader Jung Chung-rae's inappropriate behavior has created unwanted distractions from economic achievements.

Sheinbaum faces mounting pressure as approval slides from 70s to around 60% amid corruption scandals. US indictments of Sinaloa officials and CIA assassination allegations strain sovereignty claims. Consumer confidence remains depressed at 44.1. Economy contracted in Q1 2026, though Morena's congressional supermajority and social program popularity provide cushion. She's transitioning from change agent to tested incumbent.
